title: Structured Output and Schema Validation
description: Developer defines output types; agent produces and validates structured responses
use_cases: Define typed output via Python class/dataclass/BaseModel
Define fully custom output via subclassing AgentOutputSchemaBase
Resolve output schema at run time (get_output_schema)
Convert output schema to model API format (get_response_format / convert_response_format)
Send structured output format to LLM as json_schema text format
Receive raw JSON string from LLM response
Validate and parse JSON into typed object (validate_json)
Unwrap wrapped output for non-BaseModel types
Convert oneOf → anyOf for OpenAI compatibility
Expose final_output as typed object on RunResult

key_classes.name: AgentOutputSchema
key_classes.notes: Auto-wraps non-BaseModel/dict types in a TypedDict with key 'response'; calls ensure_strict_json_schema if strict=True

key_classes.name: CustomOutputSchema
key_classes.notes: Demonstrates developer-defined schema + custom validate_json logic

key_functions: get_output_schema(agent) → AgentOutputSchemaBase | None  [turn_preparation.py]: resolves agent.output_type to schema object

Converter.get_response_format(output_schema) → ResponseTextConfigParam | Omit  [openai_responses.py]: builds {format: {type: json_schema, name: final_output, schema: ..., strict: ...}} for Responses API
ChatCmplConverter.convert_response_format(output_schema) → ResponseFormat | Omit  [chatcmpl_converter.py]: builds {type: json_schema, json_schema: {name, strict, schema}} for Chat Completions API
ensure_strict_json_schema(schema) → dict  [strict_schema.py]: mutates schema to add additionalProperties:false, make all fields required, convert oneOf→anyOf, inline $refs
AgentOutputSchema.validate_json(json_str) → Any  [agent_output.py]: uses Pydantic TypeAdapter to parse, unwraps 'response' key if wrapped
output_schema.validate_json(text) called in turn_resolution.py when model returns text output and schema is non-plain-text
flow: 1. Developer sets agent.output_type = MyPydanticModel (or AgentOutputSchema or AgentOutputSchemaBase subclass)
2. Runner calls get_output_schema(agent) → creates AgentOutputSchema wrapper if needed
3. AgentOutputSchema.__init__ uses Pydantic TypeAdapter to derive JSON schema; applies ensure_strict_json_schema if strict=True
4. Model layer calls Converter.get_response_format(output_schema) → formats schema for API (json_schema text format param)
5. LLM receives schema as structured output constraint and returns JSON string
6. turn_resolution checks output_schema.is_plain_text(); if False, calls output_schema.validate_json(text)
7. validate_json uses TypeAdapter to parse; if _is_wrapped, extracts ['response'] key
8. Validated typed object stored as final_output on RunResult
key_relationships: AgentOutputSchema uses Pydantic TypeAdapter for schema derivation and validation
Agent.output_type can be: None | str | type[Any] | AgentOutputSchemaBase
important_notes: Strict mode (default=True): adds additionalProperties:false, all fields required — guarantees valid JSON from model
Non-BaseModel/dict types are auto-wrapped in TypedDict with key 'response'; unwrapped after validation
oneOf is converted to anyOf because OpenAI structured outputs do not support oneOf in nested contexts
Developer can bypass all auto-logic by subclassing AgentOutputSchemaBase directly
Plain text (None/str output_type) skips schema serialization entirely — model returns free text
Both OpenAI Responses API and Chat Completions API receive schema as 'json_schema' format type
validate_json raises ModelBehaviorError (not Python TypeError) on invalid JSON from LLM
final_output on RunResult is typed Any — handoffs can change which agent runs last
